elements.name: Hydrogen
elements.note: Hydrogen is chemically singular.
elements.hint: Use the starter preset cautiously; chemical context dominates.

elements.name: Helium
elements.note: Closed-shell structure keeps the starter assignment highly stable.
elements.hint: A robust assignment is expected unless unusual fields or bonding motifs are imposed.

elements.name: Lithium

elements.hint: A comparatively open operational gap is expected unless unusual covalency is introduced.

elements.name: Iron
elements.note: Fe often sits near competition between spin manifolds and ligand-field driven reorganisation.
elements.hint: Assignment may be control-sensitive under ligand-field variation, pressure, or oxidation-state change.

elements.name: Cobalt
elements.note: Cobalt frequently couples spin-state changes to accessible redox chemistry.
elements.hint: Both spin and oxidation-state assignments may shift.

elements.name: Nickel
elements.note: Nickel often displays coordination-sensitive electronic competition in catalysis.
elements.hint: The assignment should be checked against geometry and redox state rather than assumed rigid.

elements.name: Copper
elements.note: Copper often mixes metal-centred and ligand-centred channels.
elements.hint: Charge-transfer and redox sensitivity may both be chemically visible.

elements.name: Zinc

elements.hint: A relatively open operational gap is expected unless strong charge-transfer channels are introduced.

elements.name: Cadmium
elements.note: Closed-shell d10 behaviour often keeps the assignment comparatively robust.

elements.name: Iodine

elements.name: Xenon
elements.note: Xenon remains closed-shell but can access chemically nontrivial oxidised environments.
elements.hint: The starter assignment is robust, though heavy-atom polarisation can open reactive channels in unusual compounds.

elements.hint: A comparatively open operational gap is expected unless unusual covalency is introduced.

elements.name: Osmium
elements.note: Heavy 5d chemistry makes osmium more sensitive to spin-orbit and multiplet reordering.
elements.hint: A narrow operational corridor should be taken seriously when oxidation state and ligand field are varied.

elements.name: Iridium
elements.note: Iridium frequently couples heavy-atom spin-orbit structure to strong covalency.
elements.hint: The assignment can be control-sensitive, especially in catalytic or photochemical environments.

elements.name: Platinum
elements.note: Platinum is a heavy-element case where relativistic and spin-orbit effects can reorganise state character.
elements.hint: A narrow corridor is plausible; relativistic and charge-transfer channels should be treated explicitly.

elements.name: Gold
elements.note: Gold chemistry is strongly shaped by relativistic contraction, soft covalency, and accessible charge-transfer channels.
elements.hint: The starter model places gold in a highly sensitive corridor where small changes may alter the dominant description.

elements.name: Mercury
elements.note: Mercury reflects relativistic stabilisation of s character with softer d participation than lighter congeners.
elements.hint: The assignment is moderately stable, though relativistic effects should not be neglected.

elements.name: Thallium
elements.note: Thallium is a textbook case for inert-pair behaviour and heavy-atom reordering.
elements.hint: Operational sensitivity may arise from the balance between lower and higher oxidation states.

elements.name: Lead
elements.note: Lead commonly displays oxidation-state competition shaped by heavy-atom effects.
elements.hint: The starter assignment is moderately stable, but inert-pair chemistry can narrow the corridor.

elements.name: Bismuth
elements.note: Bismuth combines heavy-atom spin-orbit effects with oxidation-state sensitivity.
elements.hint: A moderate corridor is plausible; heavy-element reordering should be kept in view.

elements.name: Polonium
elements.note: Polonium is chemically unusual and heavy-atom effects are difficult to ignore.
elements.hint: The corridor may tighten under coordination or oxidation-state change.

elements.name: Astatine
elements.note: Astatine is strongly shaped by heavy-atom and spin-orbit effects relative to lighter halogens.
elements.hint: A narrow-to-moderate corridor is plausible in the starter model.

elements.name: Radon
elements.note: Closed-shell structure keeps the starter assignment highly stable.
elements.hint: A robust assignment is expected unless unusual fields or bonding motifs are imposed.

elements.name: Francium
elements.note: Francium remains alkali-like, but the heavy-atom limit brings stronger relativistic corrections.
elements.hint: A fairly open corridor is still expected in the starter model, with heavy-atom adjustments.

elements.name: Radium
elements.note: Radium is largely ionic in simple settings, though heavy-atom effects are stronger than in lighter congeners.
elements.hint: The starter assignment remains comparatively stable, with context-dependent heavy-atom corrections.

elements.name: Cerium

elements.hint: The corridor can tighten under redox or ligand variation.

elements.name: Europium

elements.name: Ytterbium

elements.name: Lutetium

elements.name: Actinium
elements.note: 5f, 6d, and ligand channels may compete.
elements.hint: A narrow corridor should be taken seriously.

elements.name: Thorium
elements.note: Thorium often sits near competition between actinide-centred and more delocalised channels.
elements.hint: A narrow corridor is plausible once covalency and oxidation-state variation are included.

elements.name: Protactinium

elements.hint: A narrow corridor should be taken seriously.

elements.name: Uranium
elements.note: Uranium chemistry is highly sensitive to covalency, oxidation state, and near-degenerate valence channels.
elements.hint: The assignment should be treated as control-sensitive; a narrow corridor is chemically plausible.

elements.name: Neptunium
elements.note: 5f, 6d, and ligand channels may compete.
elements.hint: A narrow corridor should be taken seriously.

elements.name: Plutonium
elements.note: Plutonium is a paradigmatic case of actinide electronic lability and delicate energetic balance.
elements.hint: A narrow corridor is strongly plausible; local state competition should be assumed rather than dismissed.

elements.name: Lawrencium
